steps.title: What Crucible is
steps.narration: Crucible is the judgment organ. You register a claim with the way it could be proven wrong, and Crucible measures it and records a verdict you can re-check.
steps.command: echo crucible - accountable judgment of ideas with witnessed verdicts
steps.stdout: crucible - accountable judgment of ideas with witnessed verdicts
steps.output: crucible - accountable judgment of ideas with witnessed verdicts

steps.title: The capability surface
steps.narration: The loop is all here: register a thesis, steelman the test that would settle it, measure against a substrate, then witness the outcome as MATCH, DRIFT, or an honest UNVERIFIABLE. It is a discipline for turning opinions into verdicts.
steps.stdout:
usage: crucible [-h] [--version]
                {status,doctor,demo,register,export,assess,steelman,measure,run,measurement-gate,refine,registry,verdicts,recheck,review,report,batch,drift,ci,mcp}
                ...

crucible: accountable judgment of ideas.

positional arguments:
  {status,doctor,demo,register,export,assess,steelman,measure,run,measurement-gate,refine,registry,verdicts,recheck,review,report,batch,drift,ci,mcp}
    status              emit Crucible's Project Telos operator-spine status
    doctor              check Crucible's operator-spine readiness
    demo                show Crucible's operator-spine demo command
    register            register a thesis (claims + falsification) into a
                        registry
    export              publication-gated export of a thesis contract
    assess              compute and witness a verdict per claim
                        (MATCH/DRIFT/UNVERIFIABLE)
    steelman            propose the test that would settle each claim (the
                        null default restates the claim's own falsification;
                        custom edges plug in through the API)
    measure             measure each claim against a substrate oracle, then
                        witness the verdicts
    run                 run a complete steelman, measurement, witnessed
                        assessment, and optional report
    measurement-gate    verify a Telos creative measurement packet
    refine              refine over rounds of substrate until the thesis is
                        cohesively verified, or report the weakest claim
    registry            inspect a stored registry: list, verify, stats,
                        search, or prune
    verdicts            list or re-check witnessed assessments in a registry
    recheck             inspect or replay oracle-level measurement descriptors
    review              validate a cleanroom review bundle before verifier
                        handoff
    report              render a Markdown report for a witnessed assessment
    batch               assess a manifest of thesis jobs into a registry
    drift               compare the latest two witnessed assessments in a
                        registry
    ci                  CI regression gate: compare a registry's verdicts
                        against a baseline, fail on regression, emit a PR-
                        comment Markdown summary
    mcp                 serve Crucible tools over MCP stdio

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --version             show program's version number and exit
steps.output:
usage: crucible [-h] [--version]
                {status,doctor,demo,register,export,assess,steelman,measure,run,measurement-gate,refine,registry,verdicts,recheck,review,report,batch,drift,ci,mcp}
                ...

steps.title: A status receipt
steps.narration: Crucible reports its own readiness as a receipt. A tool built to judge ideas fairly has to start by being transparent about itself, and that is the seal on everything it measures.
steps.stdout:
status=MATCH tool=crucible command=status
next: telos workflow - carry verified claims into the shared room
steps.output:
status=MATCH tool=crucible command=status
next: telos workflow - carry verified claims into the shared room
